non_blocking_issues: Stress test uses a local Mutex and doesn't force the intra-operation interleaving at the former seam (supplements, not an independent proof of production wiring).
apply() async -> abrupt kill can theoretically lose the last mirror write.
Prune cancel still uses key.hashCode() not channel; collision theoretical.
notes: Round-2 TOCTOU genuinely closed: both interleavings correct, Mutex held across withContext(Main.immediate) is suspend-safe (no deadlock), decision path isolated, Bug-2 logic-neutral.
